reasoning: The question asks for the probability of a specific sequence of three Fed rate decisions (Cut-Pause-Pause) for the September, October, and December 2025 FOMC meetings. My tools do not include a web search to determine what occurred at the September 2025 meeting, as my knowledge is limited to past training data and the forecast cutoff is set playfully or hypothetically in the 'future' (October 2025). Without knowledge of the September meeting outcome, calculating the ex-ante probability of any precise sequence of three Fed decisions (which has 27 possible naive permutations) yields a relatively low probability. Even assuming the Fed is in an easing cycle where cuts and pauses dominate, specific sequences like Cut-Pause-Pause compete with Cut-Cut-Cut, Cut-Pause-Cut, and Pause-Pause-Pause. Therefore, an unconditional estimate for this precise path is quite low, around 15%, reflecting the high baseline uncertainty of predicting three consecutive monetary policy decisions without current economic data.
